cards.title: Social Media Ban Improves Family Happiness
cards.body: A psychotherapist discovered that limiting her phone usage dramatically improved her mood and relationships with her children. Setting strict boundaries on social media can help frazzled parents find calm and be more present with their families.

cards.title: Parenthood Makes Your Brain Smarter
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. Years of mental and emotional demands of raising children actually boost cognitive health.

cards.title: When Picky Eating Is Actually Anxiety
cards.body: A child's food refusal may signal anxiety rather than simple pickiness. Understanding the connection between anxiety and eating can help parents address the root cause.

cards.title: Guide Adolescent Girls Through Digital Culture
cards.body: Parents can feel overwhelmed navigating toxic digital culture with their teenage daughters. Practical strategies can help safely guide adolescent girls through modern online challenges.

cards.title: Mental Health Tips Before College Starts
cards.body: Families can prepare college students for mental wellness over summer before the first semester. Practical steps from paperwork to growth mindset set students up for success.

cards.title: Permissiveness vs. Clear Limits in Parenting
cards.body: Being overly permissive with children isn't always kind or helpful. Clear, negotiated limits build security and improve understanding in parent-child relationships.
